body:
### User Story
As a developer, I want to split the large `home.md` file into separate files (`welcome.md`, `aboutme.md`, `projects.md`, and `contact.md`) so that content management is more modular and organized.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create `content/welcome.md` from the `hero` section of `home.md`.
- [ ] Create `content/aboutme.md` from the `about` section + the bottom text of `home.md`.
- [ ] Create `content/projects.md` from the `projects` section of `home.md`.
- [ ] Create `content/contact.md` from the `contact` section of `home.md`.
- [ ] Remove the `skills` section from the new structure (currently in `home.md`).
- [ ] Update `lib/markdown.ts` (specifically `getHomeContent`) to load and parse these four new files instead of `home.md`.
- [ ] Ensure `bodyHtml` returned by `getHomeContent` now comes from the content of `aboutme.md`.
- [ ] Delete or archive the original `content/home.md`.

### Technical Notes
- `welcome.md`, `projects.md`, and `contact.md` will contain mostly YAML frontmatter.
- `aboutme.md` will contain the `about` metadata and the "I am a developer..." text in its body.
- The `skills` section in `home.md` is removed as requested.
title: [Feature]: Split home.md into separate content files for modularity
